releases.author: ms3056
releases.description:
## Features
- ✨ Add sunrise and sunset times to forecast tooltips via the astro section of the weather API

## How to Use
Hover over any forecast day to see the sunrise and sunset times along with the temperature information.

## What's New
The weather API provides astronomical data including sunrise and sunset times for each day. This release exposes that data in the forecast tooltips, allowing you to see at a glance when the sun rises and sets each day.

## Technical Details
- Updated `ForecastDay` interface to include `astro` object with `sunrise` and `sunset` fields
- Enhanced `extractForecastData` method to extract sunrise/sunset times from API response
- Updated forecast tooltip to display sunrise and sunset times alongside temperature

## Compatibility
- Requires Obsidian 0.16.3+
releases.name: v0.7.9 - Sunrise & Sunset Times

releases.author: ms3056
releases.description:
**Release notes**

- Highlights: safer networking, fewer duplicate timers, clearer errors, and better defaults for reliable updates.
- Security: switches all API and icon requests to HTTPS; upgrades build tooling to address a public advisory.
- Stability: centralizes refresh timer lifecycle to prevent overlapping intervals and ensures proper cleanup.
- Defaults: enforces a 30‑minute minimum refresh interval to avoid rate‑limit issues and unnecessary network usage.
- UX: increases location input debounce and skips fetches on empty values to reduce accidental calls while typing.
- Error Handling: surfaces clear notices for missing settings and fetch failures (throttled to avoid noise).
- UI Fix: corrects layout in the weather container for consistent rendering across panes.

**Changelog**

- Security: HTTPS for WeatherAPI calls and icon URLs.
- Build: esbuild upgraded to 0.27.x; resolves advisory (GHSA-67mh-4wv8-2f99).
- Interval lifecycle: single source of interval management; avoids duplicate timers and cleans up on close.
- Settings: refresh interval clamped to ≥30 minutes; location debounce increased; empty values ignored.
- Fetch: encoded query params; improved error notices.
- UI: fixed container layout; minor AQ handling improvements.

**Notes for Obsidian users**

- After updating, set your API key and location in Tenki’s settings if prompted.
- The plugin refreshes at least every 30 minutes by design to stay within API limits.
releases.name: Tenki 0.7.8 — Security, Stability, and UX Improvements

releases.author: ms3056
releases.description:
In addition to the requested changes below I made a slight change to the refresh code. 

[function debounce(func: (...args: any[]) => void, wait: number) {](https://github.com/ms3056/Tenki/blob/73e5023325502576dadea352fffc3e4d1b2395e7/main.ts#L33-L47)
The Obsidian API already includes a function of the same name that does the same.

Response: code change implemented in 0.7.5

[updateInterval: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;](https://github.com/ms3056/Tenki/blob/73e5023325502576dadea352fffc3e4d1b2395e7/main.ts#L89)
Use a regular number here, and use window.setTimeout everywhere.

Response: code change implemented in 0.7.5

[const checkLayoutInterval = setInterval(async () => {](https://github.com/ms3056/Tenki/blob/73e5023325502576dadea352fffc3e4d1b2395e7/main.ts#L121-L127)
Remove this code.

Response: code change implemented in 0.7.5

[cls: "recent-files-donation",](https://github.com/ms3056/Tenki/blob/73e5023325502576dadea352fffc3e4d1b2395e7/main.ts#L532)
Wrong class name.
Also, the donation information is usually at the bottom of the settings.

Response: code change implemented in 0.7.5
releases.name: 0.7.6 - Small change to refresh code

Added data-app-id attribute to refresh button - all buttons where being targeted by the styling (whoops)

